legacy.menu.how_to_play.brewing_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : BREWING§f

Brewing potions requires a Brewing Stand, which can be built at a crafting table. Every potion starts off with a bottle of water, which is made by filling a Glass Bottle with water from a Cauldron or water source.
A Brewing Stand has three slots for bottles, so can be used to make three potions at the same time. One ingredient can be used over all three bottles, so always brew three potions at the same time to best use your resources.
Brewing Stands need fuel, so you'll need to acquire some Blaze Powder too.
Putting a potion ingredient in the top position of the Brewing Stand will make a base potion after a short time. This doesn't have any effect by itself, but brewing another ingredient into this base potion will give you a potion with an effect.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.brewing_message_:
Once you have this potion you can add a third ingredient to make the effect last longer (using Redstone Dust), be more intense (using Glowstone Dust), or turn into a harmful potion (using a Fermented Spider Eye).
You can also add gunpowder to any potion to turn it into a Splash Potion, which can be thrown. The thrown Splash Potion will cause the potion effect to be applied over the entire area that it lands in.
By adding Dragon's Breath to a Splash Potion, you can make a Lingering Potion which leaves a gas cloud on the the ground when thrown. Anything walking into this cloud will have the potion's effect applied to it.
The source ingredients for potions are:-
You'll need to experiment with combinations of ingredients in order to find out all of the different potions that you can make.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.chest_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : CHEST§f

Once you have crafted a Chest, you can place it in the world and then open it with %s to store items from your inventory.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.chest_message_1:
Use the pointer to move items between your inventory and the chest.

Items in the chest will be stored there for you to swap back into your inventory again later.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.conduit_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : CONDUIT§f

Conduits can be crafted with eight Nautilus Shells and one Heart of the Sea. An active Conduit will give nearby players the Conduit Power status effect, which lets them breathe underwater, see in the dark, and mine blocks faster. Players will only get Conduit Power if they are underwater or standing in the rain.

To activate a Conduit, place it underwater with every surrounding block also filled with water.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.conduit_message_:
It will also need a frame made of Prismarine Blocks, Dark Prismarine Blocks, Prismarine Bricks, or Sea Lanterns. It's fine to use a mix.

The frame should be constructed in tight rings around the nine central water blocks, with the Conduit right in the middle. The more blocks used in the frame, the larger the area the Conduit can affect. Once the frame has the maximum number of blocks (three full rings), the Conduit will also attack nearby enemies.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.crafting-table_m:
§2HOW TO PLAY : CRAFTING TABLE§f

You can craft larger items using a Crafting Table.

Place the table in the world and press %s to use it.

Crafting on a table works in the same way as basic crafting, but you have a larger crafting area, and a more varied selection of items to craft.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.crafting_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : CRAFTING§f

In the Crafting interface, you can combine items from your inventory to create new types of items. Use %s to open the crafting interface.

Scroll through the tabs at the top using %s and %s to select the type of item you wish to craft, then use %s to select the item to craft.

The crafting area shows the items required to craft the new item. Press %s to craft the item and place it in your inventory.

Scroll through the tabs on the left using %s and %s to switch between regular crafting, Banner crafting, and Firework crafting.

You can also use the 'Classic Crafting' interface by enabling the option in the settings/User interface menu. This will disable the automatic crafting of items, and instead use the method found in the Java version of Minecraft where you have to manually place ingredients in the right pattern in the grid to produce the item you want. More information on the patterns required is available on the Minecraft wiki page.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.creative: クリエイティブ モード
legacy.menu.how_to_play.creative_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : CREATIVE MODE§f

The creative mode interface allows any item in the game to be moved into the player's inventory without the need for mining or crafting the item.
The items in the player's inventory will not be removed when they are placed or used in the world. This allows the player to focus on building rather than resource gathering.

When in Creative Mode, to fly quickly tap %s twice. To fly faster, tap %s forward twice. To stop flying, double tap %s again.

When Flying, you can hold down %s to ascend and %s to descend; or use %s to move up, %s to move down, %s to move left, and %s to move right.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.dispenser_messag:
§2HOW TO PLAY : DISPENSER§f

A Dispenser is used to shoot out Items. You will need to place a switch (for example, a lever) next to the dispenser to trigger it.

To fill the dispenser with items press %s, then move the items that you want to dispense from your inventory into the dispenser.

Now whenever you use the switch, the dispenser will shout out an item.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.dropper_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : DROPPERS§f

When powered by Redstone, Droppers will drop a single random item contained within them onto the ground. Use %s to open the Dropper, and then you can load the Dropper with items from your inventory.  
If the Dropper is facing a Chest or another type of container, the item will be placed into that instead. Long chains of Droppers can be constructed to transport items over a distance, but for this to work, they will have to be alternately powered on and off.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.bees_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : BEES§f

While exploring forests and plains, you may find Bee Nests with Bees nearby. Bees fly to flowers, collect pollen, then return to their Nest or Bee Hive to make Honey.

If a pollinated Bee passes over crops, the crops can grow faster.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.bees_message_1:
When a Bee Nest or Bee Hive is full, use Shears to collect Honeycombs or use a Glass Bottle to collect a Honey Bottle.

Harvesting from a Nest or Hive can anger the Bees inside. Place a Campfire underneath it to calm them before collecting Honey or Honeycombs.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.piglins_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : PIGLINS§f

Piglins are Nether mobs that roam in groups. They attack players on sight unless the player is wearing at least one piece of Gold Armor.

Piglins like Gold Ingots. Drop or use a Gold Ingot near one and it may barter useful items in return.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.piglins_message_:
Piglins can also be found in Bastion Remnants. Piglin Brutes guard these structures and attack even if you are wearing Gold Armor.

Piglins do not like players opening or breaking containers near them, so be careful when taking loot.
Zombified Piglins are undead Piglins. They usually ignore players, but if one is attacked, nearby Zombified Piglins will become hostile too.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.netherite_messag:
§2HOW TO PLAY : NETHERITE§f

Netherite is a powerful upgrade for Diamond equipment. Mine Ancient Debris in the Nether, smelt it into Netherite Scrap, then craft four Netherite Scraps with four Gold Ingots to make a Netherite Ingot.

Ancient Debris is rare and usually found deep in the Nether. It cannot be destroyed by explosions, which can help when searching for it.

To upgrade Diamond gear, use a Smithing Table. Combine a Netherite Upgrade Smithing Template, a Diamond tool or armor piece, and a Netherite Ingot.

The result keeps its durability, name, and enchantments. Netherite gear is stronger than Diamond, resists knockback, and does not burn in fire or lava.

Netherite Upgrade Smithing Templates are found in Bastion Remnant chests and can be copied at a Crafting Table using Diamonds and Netherrack.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.trial-chamber_me:
§2HOW TO PLAY : TRIAL CHAMBERS§f

§9Trial Chambers§f
Trial Chambers are underground structures made from Copper and Tuff blocks. They are combat challenges filled with rooms, corridors, traps, decorated pots, chests, barrels, and Vaults.

Each chamber has Trial Spawners that activate when players get close.

§9Trial Spawners§f
Trial Spawners create waves of mobs based on the number of nearby players. Defeat every wave and the spawner will eject rewards before going into cooldown.

§9Breezes§f
Some rooms contain Breeze enemies. Breezes leap around the room and attack with Wind Charges that push players and can trigger buttons, trapdoors, and other blocks.

§9Vaults§f
Trial Keys can open Vaults. A Vault can reward each player once, so everyone in a group can claim loot from the same Vault.

§9Ominous Trials§f
Ominous effects can make Trial Spawners more dangerous and create Ominous Trial Keys. These open Ominous Vaults, which contain rarer rewards.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.bundle_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : BUNDLES§f

Bundles are storage items that can hold mixed item stacks in one inventory slot. They are useful while exploring, mining, or building because small leftover stacks can be packed together.

Craft a Bundle with String and Leather. Dyed Bundles work the same way and can help you color-code supplies.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.bundle_message_1:
A Bundle can hold up to one full stack of items. Most items use one space each, items that normally stack to 16 use four spaces each, and non-stackable items fill the Bundle by themselves.

Use %s to put items into a Bundle. Use %s on a Bundle to remove the selected item.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.bundle_message_2:
The Bundle tooltip shows what is inside and how full it is. If several item types are visible, use %s to choose which item will come out next.

Some container items cannot be packed inside Bundles. When holding a filled Bundle in the world, use %s to release items from it.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.pale-garden_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : THE PALE GARDENS§f

The Pale Gardens are quiet forest biomes filled with Pale Oak trees, Pale Moss, Pale Moss Carpets, Pale Hanging Moss, and Eyeblossoms.

Some Pale Oak trees contain Creaking Hearts. At night, an active Creaking Heart can spawn a Creaking.

The Creaking is a hostile mob linked to a Creaking Heart. It moves to attack players, but cannot move while a Survival or Adventure player is looking at it.

To defeat a Creaking, find and destroy the Creaking Heart it is connected to.

Creakings only appear at night and only when a Creaking Heart is active. Not every Pale Oak tree contains one, so some Pale Gardens may be safer than others.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.autocrafter_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : AUTOCRAFTER§f

An Autocrafter can craft items automatically when it receives a Redstone signal. Use %s to open it, then place ingredients into its grid like a Crafting Table.

When powered, it crafts one item from the recipe and pushes the result into the output slot.

Slots can be toggled on or off so the Autocrafter only accepts items in the spaces needed by the recipe.

Hoppers and Droppers can feed ingredients in and pull the crafted item out, making it useful for automatic farms and Redstone machines.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.elytra_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : ELYTRA§f

Elytra are an item that can be equipped to allow gliding. Elytra are equipped in the chest slot, as if they were a piece of armor.

Elytra cannot be crafted but are found in item frames in rare End Ships, guarded by fearsome Shulkers.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.elytra_message_1:
When equipped with Elytra, a player can press %s while falling to start gliding.
Note that you need to be quite high to really get the full effect. While gliding, you can steer by looking around. If you look downwards, you'll move faster but will also hit the ground sooner. If you look upwards, you'll gain some height, but if you do it for too long, you'll stall and start falling instead of rising!
As you fly, your Elytra will lose durability. You can repair them by combining them with a Phantom Membrane at an Anvil. If they break mid-flight, you'll fall, so be careful.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.enchantment_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : ENCHANTMENT§f

The Experience Points collected by picking up Experience Orbs can be used to enchant Swords, Bows, Axes, Pickaxes, Shovels, Hoes, Armor, Tridents, Maces, and Books.
When an Enchantable item is placed in the slot below the book in the Enchantment Table, the three buttons to the right of the slot will display some enchantments with their Experience Level cost and Lapis Lazuli cost.
If you do not have enough Experience Levels or have not provided enough Lapis Lazuli to use some of these, the relevant cost will appear in red; otherwise, it will be shown in green.

The actual enchantment applied is randomly selected based on the cost displayed.

If the Enchantment Table is surrounded by Bookshelves (up to a maximum of 15), with a one block gap between the Bookcase and the Enchantment Table, Arcane glyphs will be seen coming from the book on the Enchantment Table and the potency of the enchantments will be increased.

All of the ingredients for an Enchantment Table can be found within the villages in a world, or by mining and cultivation of the world.

Enchanted Books are used at the Anvil to apply enchantments to items. This gives you more control over which enchantments you would like on your items.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.end-cities_messa:
§2HOW TO PLAY : END CITIES§f

After killing the Ender Dragon, an End Gateway Portal will generate. Throwing an Ender Pearl into the portal will teleport you to an outer End island. End Cities spawn on outer End islands and contain some very valuable loot.

Exploring End islands is dangerous, though, so be sure to pack plenty of food and other useful items like Ender Pearls.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.archaeology_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : ARCHAEOLOGY§f

§9Brushing§f
Suspicious Sand and Suspicious Gravel can be found in structures such as Desert Pyramids, Desert Wells, Ocean Ruins, and Trail Ruins.

Use a Brush on these blocks carefully. Brushing reveals the item hidden inside, but breaking the block normally destroys the contents.

§9Decorated Pots§f
Some suspicious blocks contain Pottery Sherds. Combine four Sherds, Bricks, or both in a Crafting Table to make a Decorated Pot.

Each Sherd changes one side of the pot, letting you choose the pattern.

§9Sniffer Eggs§f
Warm Ocean Ruins can contain Sniffer Eggs. Hatch them to raise Sniffers, which can dig up ancient seeds.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.deep-dark_messag:
§2HOW TO PLAY : THE DEEP DARK§f

The Deep Dark can be found deep underground. They are uniquely covered in Sculk, and Ancient Cities may generate there. They contain very valuable loot such as unique enchantments and armor trims.

Sculk Sensors react to nearby sounds and can send signals to Sculk Shriekers. Sneaking and walking on Wool can help you to keep quiet.

If a Shrieker is triggered 4 or more times, a Warden will emerge. The Warden is extremely dangerous and cannot be killed easily, so avoiding it is usually the best plan.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.sculk-sensor_mes:
§2HOW TO PLAY : SCULK SENSOR§f

Sculk Sensors are redstone components found in the Deep Dark. They listen for nearby vibrations, such as footsteps, blocks being broken, projectiles landing, containers opening, or mobs moving.

When a vibration is detected, the sensor lights up and emits a redstone signal. The closer the vibration is, the stronger the signal.

Wool blocks can block vibrations, and placing Wool around a sensor lets you control which directions it can hear from.

Calibrated Sculk Sensors work like normal sensors, but can be tuned with a redstone signal. The signal strength sent into the input side is the only vibration frequency the sensor will listen for.

The calibration strengths are:-
legacy.menu.how_to_play.sculk-sensor_cal:
* §91 - Movement§f
* §92 - Landing§f
* §93 - Item interactions§f
* §94 - Elytra gliding or unique mob actions§f
* §95 - Dismounting or equipping gear§f
* §96 - Mounting or mob interactions§f
* §97 - Damage§f
* §98 - Eating or drinking§f
* §99 - Blocks deactivating or closing§f
* §910 - Blocks activating or opening§f
* §911 - Blocks changing§f
* §912 - Blocks being destroyed§f
* §913 - Blocks being placed§f
* §914 - Spawning, teleporting, or lightning§f
* §915 - Death or explosions§f
legacy.menu.how_to_play.sculk-sensor_mes:
Blocks of Amethyst can be used for vibration resonance. Place one beside a Sculk Sensor or Calibrated Sculk Sensor, and when the sensor detects a vibration, the Block of Amethyst re-emits that frequency as a new vibration from its own position.

This makes Sculk Sensors useful for wireless redstone, hidden doors, traps, alarm systems, and contraptions that react to movement without needing pressure plates or tripwires.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.armor-trims_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : ARMOR TRIMS§f

Armor Trims let you decorate armor with different patterns and colors. Find Smithing Templates in structures throughout the world, then combine a template, an armor piece, and a material in a Smithing Table.

The template controls the pattern, and the material controls the trim color.

Armor Trims are cosmetic and do not change how strong the armor is.

Each Smithing Template can be copied at a Crafting Table using Diamonds and the block shown on its tooltip, so you can keep using a rare template once you find it.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.happy-ghast_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : HAPPY GHAST§f

Happy Ghasts are friendly flying mobs grown from Dried Ghasts. A Dried Ghast can be found in the Nether and placed in water to become a Ghastling.

Feed the Ghastling Snowballs to help it grow into a Happy Ghast.

Happy Ghasts can be equipped with a Harness so players can ride them through the sky. Press %s while looking at a Happy Ghast to mount it.

When a player stands on top of a Happy Ghast, its body becomes solid, letting it act as a flying platform.

Harnesses can be dyed different colors, and multiple players can ride together.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.copper-golem_mes:
§2HOW TO PLAY : COPPER GOLEM§f

Copper Golems are small helpers that sort items. To make one, place a Carved Pumpkin or Jack-o-Lantern on top of a Copper Block.

Drop items into a Copper Chest, and a Copper Golem will take them out and try to place them into nearby normal Chests.

If a Chest already contains the same item, the Copper Golem will sort matching items there. Empty Chests can be used for items that do not have a matching Chest yet.

Like other Copper blocks, Copper Golems oxidize over time. When fully oxidized, they become Copper Golem Statues. Use an Axe to scrape away oxidation, or use Honeycomb to preserve their current state.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ender-chest_mess: All Ender Chests in a world are united. Items placed into an Ender Chest are accessible in any other. However, the contents of the Ender Chests are different for each player. This allows players to store items in any Ender Chest, and retrieve them from other Ender Chests in different positions in the world.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.farming-animals_:
§2HOW TO PLAY : FARMING ANIMALS§f

If you want to keep your animals in one place, build a fenced area of less than 20x20 blocks and have your animals inside it. This ensures that they will still be there when you come back to see them.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.firework_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : FIREWORKS§f

Fireworks are single-use decorative items that can be launched by hand or from Dispensers. They are crafted with Paper and Gunpowder, but you can also add Firework Stars to give your Fireworks awesome explosions!
The colors, fade, shape, size, and effects (such as trails and twinkle) of Firework Stars can be customized by including additional ingredients when crafting.

You can craft a Firework or Firework Star in the standard 2x2 Crafting interface, but using a Crafting Table will let you add more ingredients for more impressive displays. When in either crafting interface, switch to the Fireworks type by pressing %s twice.

First, create the Firework Stars you want by adding ingredients with %s to change the color, shape, and effect of the Firework Star, then press %s to create a Firework Star.
- A Dye will set the color of the explosion of the Firework Star.
- A Fire Charge, Gold Nugget, Feather, or Mob Head will set the shape of the explosion.
- A trail or a twinkle can be added using Diamonds or Glowstone Dust.

Remember that you don't need any Firework Stars to craft a Firework, but they are nice to have. When you're done, hit %s to move on.

Next, you can add Fade to as many Firework Stars as you'd like. Select the Firework Star you want to add a Fade to, then add dyes with %s to make that Firework Star fade to that color after exploding.  
When you've added as much Fade as you like, press %s to create the star, then %s to move to the Firework Rocket Group.

You can choose to add as many Firework Stars as you like to the crafting grid to include them in the Firework.

Filling more slots in the crafting grid with Gunpowder will increase the height at which all of the Firework Stars will explode.

Once you're done, press %s to craft your completed Firework.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.furnace_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : FURNACE§f

A Furnace allows you to change items by firing them. For example, you can smelt Iron Ore into Iron Ingots in the Furnace.

Place the Furnace in the world and press %s to use it.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.furnace_message_:
You need to put some fuel into the bottom of the furnace, and the item to be fired in the top. The furnace will then fire up and start working.

When your items have been fired, you can move them from the output area into your inventory.

If an item your inventory pointer is over is an ingredient or fuel for the furnace, you can quick move it to the correct slot by pressing %s.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.hopper_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : HOPPERS§f

Hoppers are used to insert or remove items from containers and to automatically pick up items thrown into them.  
They can affect Brewing Stands, Chests, Dispensers, Droppers, Minecarts with Chests, Minecarts with Hoppers, and other Hoppers.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.hopper_message_1:
Hoppers will continuously attempt to suck items out of a suitable container placed above them. They will also attempt to insert stored items into an output container. If a Hopper is powered by Redstone, it will become inactive and stop both sucking and inserting items.

A Hopper tries to output items in the direction it is pointing. To make a Hopper point to a particular block, place the Hopper against that block while sneaking.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.horses_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : HORSES§f

Horses and Donkeys are found mainly in open plains and savannas. A Mule is the offspring of a Donkey and a Horse and cannot breed. Llamas can only be found in savannas and extreme hills.
A player can ride any adult Horse, Donkey, Mule, or Llama. Only Horses can be armored, and only Mules, Donkeys, and Llamas can be equipped with saddlebags for transporting items.
By using a Lead on a Llama, other Llamas will naturally follow and form long caravans that can haul large quantities of items at once. Llamas can also be clothed with carpets.
Horses, Donkeys, Mules, and Llamas must be tamed before they can be used. A player can tame a horse by riding it and staying on while it tries to buck them off. When Love Hearts appear around the horse, it has been tamed and will no longer attempt to throw the player off. To steer a horse, the player must equip it with a Saddle.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.horses_message_1:
Press %s to dismount from a horse.

Saddles can be bought from villagers or found inside chests hidden in the world. Tamed Donkeys, Mules, and Llamas can be given saddlebags by attaching a Chest to them. These saddlebags can then be accessed while riding or sneaking.

Horses and Donkeys (but not Mules) can be bred like other animals using Golden Apples or Golden Carrots. Llamas can be bred using Hay Blocks.
Foals will grow into adults over time, although feeding them Wheat or Hay will speed this up.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.host-and-player-: ホストとプレイヤーのオプション

§9Bonus Chest§f
When enabled, a chest containing some useful items will be created near the player spawn point.

§9Can Attack Animals§f
This option is only available when "Trust Players" is turned off. When this option is disabled the player will not be able to  cause damage to animals.

§9Can Attack Players§f
This option is only available when "Trust Players" is turned off. When this option is disabled the player will not be able to  cause damage to other players.

§9Can Build and Mine§f
This option is only available when "Trust Players" is turned off. When this option is enabled, the player is able to interact with the world as normal. When disabled the player will not be able to place or destroy blocks, or interact with many items and blocks.

§9Can Fly§f
When this option is enabled, the player is able to fly. This option is only relevant to Survival mode, as flying is enabled for all players in Creative mode.

§9Can Open Containers§f
This option is only available when "Trust Players" is turned off. When this option is disabled the player will not be able to open containers, such as chests.

§9Can Teleport§f
This allows the player to move players or themselves to other players in the world.

§9Can Use Doors and Switches§f
This option is only available when "Trust Players" is turned off. When this option is disabled the player will not be able to use doors and switches.

§9Daylight Cycle§f
When disabled, the time of day will not change.

§9Disable Exhaustion§f
This option only affects Survival mode. When enabled, physical activities (walking/sprinting/jumping etc.) do not consume §f🍖§f. However, if the player becomes injured, the §f🍖§f bar will slowly deplete while the player is healing.

§9Fire Spreads§f
When enabled, fire may spread to nearby flammable blocks. This option can also be changed from within the game.

§9Game Mode§f
This allows the player to be in a different game mode from other players in the game.

§4Game Options§f
When loading or creating a world, you can press the "More Options" button to enter a menu that allows more control over your game.

§9Generate Structures§f
When enabled, structures such as Villages and Strongholds will generate in the world.

§9Host Options§f
The host player, and any player set as moderators, can access the "Host Options" menu. In this menu they can enable and disable fire spreading and TNT exploding, and change the time of day, gamemode, difficulty, and other options.

§4Host Player Options§f
If "Host Privileges" is enabled, the host player can modify some privileges for themselves. To modify the privileges for a player, select their name and press %s to bring up the player privileges menu where you can use the following options.

§9Host Privileges§f
When enabled, the host can toggle their ability to fly, disable exhaustion, and make themselves invisible from the in-game menu. This option disables achievments for this world while playing, and if loading it again after saving with this option on.

§4Ingame Options§f
While in the game a number of options can be accessed by pressing %s to bring up the in-game menu.

§9Invisible§f
When this option is enabled the player cannot be seen by other players, and is invulnerable.

§9Keep Inventory§f
When enabled, players will keep their inventory when they die.

§9Kick Player§f
For players that are not on the same device as the host player, selecting this option will kick the player from the game and any other players on their device. This player will not be able to rejoin the game until it is restarted.
§2HOW TO PLAY : HOST AND PLAYER OPTIONS§f

§9Mob Griefing§f
When disabled, monsters and animals will be unable to change blocks (for example: Creeper explosions won't destroy blocks, and Sheep won't remove Grass) or pick up items.

§9Mob Loot§f
When disabled, monsters and animals will not drop loot (for example, Creepers won't drop gunpowder).

§9Mob Spawning§f
When disabled, mobs will not spawn naturally.

§9Moderator§f
When this option is enabled, the player is able to kick players, and enable and disable fire spreading and TNT exploding. If "Trust Players" is turned off, the player will also be able to change privileges for other players (except the host).

§9Natural Regeneration§f
When disabled, players will not regenerate health naturally.

§4Player Options§f
To modify the privileges for a player, select their name and press %s to bring up the player privileges menu where you can use the following options.

§9Player vs Player§f
When enabled, players can inflict damage on others players. This option only affects Survival and Adventure modes.

§9Reset The End§f
When enabled, a new End will be generated. This is useful if you have an older save where End Cities were not present.

§9Reset Nether§f
When enabled, a new Nether will be generated. This is useful if you have an older save where Nether Fortresses were not present.

§9Set Player Spawn Point§f
This changes where the player will respawn after they die.

§9Superflat World§f
When selected, a completely flat world will be generated in the Overworld and in the Nether. This option completely disables achievements for this world while playing, and if loading it again after saving with this option on.

§9Tile Drops§f
When disabled, blocks will not drop items when destroyed (for example, Stone blocks won't drop Cobblestone).

§9TNT Explodes§f
When enabled, TNT will explode when detonated. This option can also be changed from within the game.

§9Trust Players§f
When disabled, players joining the game are restricted in what they can do. They are not able to mine or use items, place blocks, use doors and switches, use containers, attack players, or attack animals. You can change these options for a specific player using the in-game menu.

§9Vines Spread§f
When enabled, vines may spread to nearby blocks. Does not affect other type of vine blocks such as Weeping Vines, Twisting Vines, and Cave Vines.

§9Weather Cycle§f
When disabled, the weather will not change.

§4World Generation Options§f
When creating a new world there are some additional options.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.hud_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : HUD§f

The HUD shows information about your status; your health, your remaining oxygen, your hunger level, and your armor (if you are wearing any).

If you lose some health but have 9 or more 🍖, your health will automatically replenish. Eating food will replenish your 🍖.
The Experience Bar is also shown here. The number shows  your current Experience Level, and the bar indicates how many Experience Orbs are required to increase your Experience Level.
Experience Orbs are gained by collecting Experience Orbs. You can get Experience orbs by:
legacy.menu.how_to_play.hud_message_1:
- Killing Mobs
- Mining certain block types
- Breeding animals
- Fishing
- Smelting ore in a furnace

The hotbar shows the items that are available to use. Use %s and %s to change the item in your hand.
Some items can be placed in your offhand slot for quick use; Maps will allow you to look at them while using something else, and Arrows in offhand slot will be used first.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.inventory_messag:
§2HOW TO PLAY : INVENTORY§f

Press %s to view your inventory.

This screen shows the items that are available for use in your hand, and all of other items that you are carrying. Your armor is also shown here.

Use %s to move the pointer.

Use %s to pick up the item that is under the pointer. If there is more than one item here this will pick them all up, or you can use %s to pick up just half of them.

You can place items down again by using %s. With multiple items on the pointer, use %s to place them all or %s to place just one.

If the item you are pointing at is armor, you can quick move it to the correct armor slot by pressing %s.

It is possible to change the color of Leather Armor by dyeing it. You can do this in the inventory menu by picking up the dye you want to use then pressing %s while the pointer is over the piece of armor you want to dye.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.large-chest_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : LARGE CHEST§f

Two chests placed next to each other will be combined to form a large chest. This can store even more items.
It is used in the same way as a normal chest.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.multiplayer_hot-: If you are playing in a High Definition mode, you can have local players join your game by attaching controllers and pressing START at any point during the game.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.multiplayer_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : MULTIPLAYER§f

Minecraft Java is a multiplayer game by default. You can join servers by going to Play Game and Join.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.multiplayer_worl:
When you start or join an online game, it will be visible to people in your friends list.
When you are in a game, you can press the %s button to bring up a list of all other players in the game, and Kick players from the game.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.nether-portal_me:
§2HOW TO PLAY : NETHER PORTAL§f

A Nether Portal allows players to travel between the Overworld and The Nether. The Nether can be used to fast-travel in the Overworld; traveling one block distance in The Nether is equivalent to traveling 8 blocks in the Overworld.
This means that if you build a second portal in The Nether and exit through it, you will be 8 times further away from your entry point than the distance that you walked.

A minimum of 10 Obsidian blocks are required to build a portal, and the portal needs to be at least 5 blocks high by 4 blocks wide.
Portals can be up to 23 blocks high by 23 blocks wide as long as they are rectangular.

Once the frame is built, the portal can be activated by igniting the interior of the frame. This can be done using Flint and Steel or a Fire Charge.

Examples of portal construction are shown in the picture to the right.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_dolph:
§9Dolphins§f
Dolphins are clever and sociable animals that will swim with you if they're nearby. They may even help you go faster!
Feeding raw Cod to a Dolphin might even make it want to help you in other ways... they know where everything is on the Ocean floor.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_drown:
§9Drowned§f
When a Zombie spends enough time underwater, it becomes one of the Drowned; a terrifying monster that can swim after its target, or hurl Tridents if it has found any.
Drowned can drop Nautilus Shells, which can be crafted together with a Heart of the Sea into a Conduit.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_fish:
§9Fish§f
There are a few types of fish in Minecraft, each with their own distinct flavor. Salmon swim up rivers, Cod travel together in shoals, Tropical fish have a huge variety of color and species, and Pufferfish will poison anyone that gets too close!
Fish can even be scooped up in Buckets for safe transportation.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_guard:
§9Guardians§f
Guardians are very powerful defenders of Ocean Monuments that attack with their laser eye beams!
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_nauti:
§9Nautilus§f
Nautiluses are peaceful ocean mounts that can be tempted and tamed with Pufferfish. Once saddled, a Nautilus can carry you quickly through the water and dash forward while you ride.
Riding a Nautilus gives you Breath of the Nautilus, which stops your air from running out while you explore underwater.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_sea-t:
§9Sea Turtles§f
Sea Turtles travel in the Oceans, swimming very far but always returning home to lay their eggs.
Sea Turtles can be bred by feeding them Sea Grass, which can be found on the Ocean floor.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_squid:
§9Squid§f
Squid casually float about, minding their own business - until attacked, when they squirt out a cloud of ink to escape!
legacy.menu.how_to_play.ocean-mobs_zombi:
§9Zombie Nautilus§f
Zombie Nautiluses are undead ocean mounts that can appear with Drowned riders. If you knock the rider off, you can tempt and tame the Zombie Nautilus with Pufferfish.
Some Zombie Nautiluses have coral-covered shells, but they behave the same as other Zombie Nautiluses.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.oceans_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : OCEANS§f

There are lots of different kinds of oceans in Minecraft; they can be warm, cold, deep, or even frozen!

Each ocean type has different plant and animal life.
Warm oceans are filled with beautiful coral reefs teeming with tropical fish; be careful with coral though, as it needs water to survive.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.oceans_message_1:
Many oceans will contain shipwrecks and underwater ruins, which are filled with chests full of powerful loot for the taking if you can breathe underwater. You may even find a map to buried treasure!

Only the deepest oceans contain ocean monuments, large underwater temples protected by powerful Guardians!
legacy.menu.how_to_play.scaffolding_mess:
§2HOW TO PLAY : SCAFFOLDING§f

Scaffolding can help you create huge structures safely. You can walk inside Scaffolding and stand on it.
If you have a column of Scaffolding you can climb it by standing in the middle and holding the %s button.
If you want to climb down again, hold the %s button.

Scaffolding needs support. You can only place 4 Scaffolding blocks horizontally without them touching a different block.
When you attempt to place the 5th Scaffolding block without proper support, it will fall.
Scaffolding can be built up into huge towers very quickly without moving - placing a Scaffolding block on top of another will place it at the very top of the pile, rather than just on top of that block.

When Scaffolding is broken it will also break every Scaffolding block that was being supported by it.
This will let you quickly clean up your Scaffolding by breaking the supporting blocks!
legacy.menu.how_to_play.raids_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : RAIDS§f

While exploring the world you may encounter a Pillager encampment, or patrol. Pillagers are always looking for villages to raid.
Pillager Captains carry banners. Killing one will drop an Ominous Bottle. These range from levels I to V, and when drunk will give you the Bad Omen status effect. 
Entering a village with the Bad Omen status effect will trigger a Raid, and waves of enemies will start attacking the village.

Defeating all of the raiders will end the Raid, and you will gain the Hero of the Village status effect.
Villagers tend to be very grateful to their heroes, so be sure to check back with them. You might get a good deal on some trades.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.the-end_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : THE END§f

The End is another dimension that can be reached through an active End Portal. The End Portal can be found in a Stronghold, which is deep underground in the Overworld.
To activate the End Portal, you'll need to put an Eye of Ender into every End Portal Frame block that doesn't have one. Once the portal is active, jump into it to go to The End.

In The End, you will meet the Ender Dragon, a fierce and powerful enemy, along with many Endermen. Make sure you are well-prepared for the battle before going there!
legacy.menu.how_to_play.the-end_message_:
Atop ten pillars of Obsidian, there are Ender Crystals that the Ender Dragon uses to heal itself. The first step in the battle is to destroy each of these. The first few can be reached with arrows, but several are protected by an Iron Fence cage; you will need to build up to them.

While you are doing this, the Ender Dragon will be attacking by flying at you and spitting balls of Ender acid. If you approach the Egg Podium in the center of the spikes, the Ender Dragon will fly down and attack you. This is where you can really do some damage to it! Avoid the acid breath and target the Ender Dragon's eyes for the highest damage. If possible, bring some friends into The End to help you with the battle!

Once you are in The End, your friends will be able to see the location of the End Portal within the Stronghold on their maps, so they can easily join you.
legacy.menu.how_to_play.trading_message:
§2HOW TO PLAY : TRADING§f

It is possible to trade items with villagers. Each villager has a profession; they can be Farmers, Butchers, Blacksmiths, Librarians, or Priests, and this affects the type of items they might trade. You can find a list of all the trades a villager is offering in the trading menu. A villager may modify or add to its trades whenever a player trades with it, although a trade might become temporarily disabled if it is used too frequently.
Trades usually involve buying or selling a number of items for emeralds.
If you do not have the items required for a trade, the items are shown in red.
